summary: Little changed in hospital AI workflow adoption today, so the main signal is continuity: documentation and patient-flow pilots still depend on integration proof before they scale.
overview: The daily notebook rule keeps the archive complete even on quiet days. No major new hospital workflow signal is recorded for this date, but the standing adoption pattern remains useful: health systems are watching AI tools for documentation burden, throughput, revenue operations, bed capacity, policy readiness, and clinical decision support while asking whether the tools fit existing care-team routines.

challengeLog.biggestStoppingPoint: Integration is the biggest stopping point today: quiet news does not remove the need to connect AI outputs to EHR, staffing, routing, revenue, and governance systems before hospitals can rely on them at scale.
items: Trust remains tied to source quality, auditability, and clinician review.
Workflow fit is still the practical test for whether teams use the tool after a pilot.
Scaling depends on repeatable deployment patterns across units, sites, and roles.
